flags.concern: No `.migrations_staging/` directory exists in the repo and there are zero `.sql` files anywhere. The plan incorrectly claims this is the established migration convention. The executor will need to either create it from scratch or locate the actual migration tooling (Supabase CLI, dashboard migrations, etc.) before writing the schema changes.
flags.evidence: Repository search for `.migrations/`, `migrations/`, `supabase/migrations/`, and `.sql` files returns zero results. No migration tooling is present.

flags.concern: `scripts/send_social_picks.py` calls `db_handler.get_recent_live_update_feed_items(guild_id, live_channel_id, limit)` with positional arguments. Adding an `environment` parameter to that method (before or between existing params) will shift bindings and break this script at runtime.
flags.evidence: Addressed FLAGS 2-5 by (a) mandating append-only `environment` kwarg placement on every signature so `send_social_picks.py:116` and similar keyword callers stay intact, (b) rewriting all remaining `self.dry_run` references in `live_top_creations.py:326,404,434` and `live_update_editor.py:1347` to `self.environment == "dev"` with a final-grep gate, (c) changing the migration to drop and recreate composite unique constraints `(environment, checkpoint_key)` and `(environment, duplicate_key)` so upserts no longer collide across environments, and (d) requiring the manual pre-insert `(natural_key, guild_id)` lookups in `store_live_top_creation_post`, `upsert_live_update_editorial_memory`, and `upsert_live_update_watchlist` to also filter by `environment`. FLAG-001 was rejected after re-verification — `.migrations_staging/` exists with 4 SQL files; the reviewer's repo scan was wrong. Added a runtime-grep audit step alongside the existing storage-grep audit to catch any missed `self.dry_run` references.

flags.concern: `self.dry_run` is referenced in `_publish_candidate`, `_resolve_top_channel_id`, `_resolve_art_channel_id`, and `_resolve_live_channel_id`. The plan deletes `dry_run` from constructors but does not clearly mandate replacing these references with `self.environment == 'dev'` checks. This will raise `AttributeError` at runtime.

flags.concern: Checkpoint and duplicate-state upserts use single-column `on_conflict` keys (`checkpoint_key`, `duplicate_key`). Adding `environment` to the payload without changing the DB unique constraints means dev upserts will match and overwrite prod rows (or vice versa). The migration must change unique constraints to `(environment, checkpoint_key)` and `(environment, duplicate_key)`.
flags.evidence: Addressed FLAGS 2-5 by (a) mandating append-only `environment` kwarg placement on every signature so `send_social_picks.py:116` and similar keyword callers stay intact, (b) rewriting all remaining `self.dry_run` references in `live_top_creations.py:326,404,434` and `live_update_editor.py:1347` to `self.environment == "dev"` with a final-grep gate, (c) changing the migration to drop and recreate composite unique constraints `(environment, checkpoint_key)` and `(environment, duplicate_key)` so upserts no longer collide across environments, and (d) requiring the manual pre-insert `(natural_key, guild_id)` lookups in `store_live_top_creation_post`, `upsert_live_update_editorial_memory`, and `upsert_live_update_watchlist` to also filter by `environment`. FLAG-001 was rejected after re-verification — `.migrations_staging/` exists with 4 SQL files; the reviewer's repo scan was wrong. Added a runtime-grep audit step alongside the existing storage-grep audit to catch any missed `self.dry_run` references.

flags.concern: `store_live_top_creation_post`, `upsert_live_update_editorial_memory`, and `upsert_live_update_watchlist` perform manual pre-insert lookups (`duplicate_key`+`guild_id`, `memory_key`+`guild_id`, `watch_key`+`guild_id`) to decide update-vs-insert. None of these lookups are scoped by `environment` in the plan, so a dev run could update an existing prod row instead of inserting a separate dev row.
